description: Standard four-dimension rubric for code-review evaluation: finds_bug (objective — did the LLM identify the seeded vulnerability?), severity_accuracy (subjective — did it rate severity correctly?), actionability (subjective — did it give concrete fix steps?), and no_false_alarms (objective — did it avoid inventing extra bugs?). Scored 1-5; aggregation: weighted_mean with overall pass at >= 4.

dimensions.name: finds_bug
dimensions.description: Does the review correctly identify the seeded vulnerability? Score 5 if the LLM names the specific vulnerability class; 3 if it identifies the vulnerable area but mislabels it; 1 if it misses the vulnerability entirely.

dimensions.name: severity_accuracy
dimensions.description: How accurately does the LLM rate the severity? Compare against the expected true_severity in the reference. Score 5 if exactly correct, 3 if off by one tier (e.g., said Medium when truly High), 1 if rated Low or not-a-concern when truly High or Critical.

dimensions.name: actionability
dimensions.description: Did the LLM give concrete, specific remediation steps that a developer could act on directly? Specific code patterns or named techniques score 5; vague guidance like 'sanitize input' alone scores 1-2.

dimensions.name: no_false_alarms
dimensions.description: Did the LLM avoid inventing additional bugs that aren't actually present in the code? Score 5 if the review only flags real issues. Penalize spurious 'might also consider...' findings that aren't grounded in the actual code down toward 1.
